segments.text: Part 4C: Membranes and steroids

Membranes
Cell membranes act as barriers
Transport is highly regulated:
Passive diffusion: some gases
Facilitated transport: metabolites, using transported
Active transport: requires energy
Membranes are made up mostly of phospholipids
Two asymmetric leaflets in the membrane

Cell Membranes B. Transport Across a Cell Membrane
Small molecules like O 2 and CO 2 can diffuse through the cell membrane, traveling from higher to lower concentration.
Larger polar molecules need facilitated transport (facilitated diffusion) to cross efficiently. Glucose.
Ions like Cl − or HCO 3 − travel through integral protein channels .
Other ions, Na + , K + , and Ca 2+ , move against the concentration gradient ; this required energy input and is called active transport .

Cell Membranes A. Structure of the Cell Membrane
Phospholipid bilayer
2 Leaflets

19.6	Phospholipids A. Phosphoacylglycerols (phospholipids)
3 C of
Glycerol
Polar head
Phopsphatidyl choline
19.7	Cell Membranes A. Structure of the Cell Membrane

Membranes: phospholipids
Major phospholipids in Eukaryotes
The membrane is asymmetric:
outer leaflet composition is different from the inner leaflet

Cell Membranes Structure of the Cell Membrane
Proteins and cholesterol molecules are embedded in the lipid bilayer membrane.
Peripheral proteins are embedded within the membrane and extend outward on one side only.
Integral proteins extend through the entire bilayer.
Sometimes carbohydrates are attached to the exterior of the cell forming glycolipids and glycoproteins .

19.7	Cell Membranes A. Structure of the Cell Membrane
Sometimes carbohydrates are attached to the exterior of the cell forming glycolipids and glycoproteins .
The ABO blood types are based on the types of glycoproteins on the surface of red blood cells

24.4 How Is Cholesterol Synthesized?
Cholesterol is the most prevalent steroid in animals
Plants do not contain cholesterol, but they DO contain steroids very similar in structure
Cholesterol is a crucial component of cell membranes, required for maintaining membrane rigidity
Cholesterol is a precursor to all steroid hormones
Liver is the primary site of cholesterol synthesis

24.4 How Is Cholesterol Synthesized?
Figure 24.30 The structure of cholesterol

24.4 How Is Cholesterol Synthesized?
Figure 24.30 The structure of cholesterol drawn in a form that more accurately describes the conformation of the ring system.

24.4 – How Is Cholesterol Synthesized?
Biosynthesis from acetyl- CoA units
First step is a thiolase reaction
Second step makes HMG-CoA
Third step - HMG-CoA reductase - is the rate-limiting step in cholesterol biosynthesis
HMG -CoA reductase is site of action of cholesterol-lowering drugs

Synthesis of HMG-CoA
Thiolase combines 2 units of acetyl-CoA
Product is 4 C unit: acetoacetyl -	CoA
2.  HMG-CoA synthase: combines acetoacetyl -CoA (4 C) with another acetyl-CoA to make HMG-CoA       (6 C)

Mevalonate Is Synthesized from Acetyl-CoA Via HMG-CoA Reductase
Step 3:
HMG -CoA undergoes two NADPH-dependent reductions to produce 3 R - mevalonate
This is the rate limiting step
HMG-CoA reductase
It is highly regulated
Targeted by cholesterol-lowering drugs

Mevalonate Is Synthesized from Acetyl-CoA Via HMG-CoA Reductase
Figure 24.32b The structure of HMG-CoA reductase.

Regulation of HMG-CoA Reductase
Three different mechanisms contribute to the regulation of HMG- CoA reductase
Phosphorylation by kinase inactivates HMG-CoA reductase .  This inactivation can be reversed by phosphatases
Degradation of HMG- CoA reductase .  This enzyme has a half-life of only 3 hours, and the half-life itself depends on cholesterol levels:  High [cholesterol] means a short half-life for HMG- CoA reductase
Gene expression.  Cholesterol levels control the amount of mRNA.  If [cholesterol] is high, levels of mRNA coding for the reductase are reduced

The conversion of mevalonate to squalene
Don ’ t worry about the details of the reactions
Remember that squalene is an important intermediate

Squalene Is Synthesized from Mevalonate
Squalene is a long branched Carbon chain with a precise arrangement of double bonds
The double bonds can be used to form new C-C bonds

Cholesterol is Formed from Squalene – After Many Steps
At the endoplasmic reticulum membrane
Squalene monooxygenase converts squalene to squalene-2,3-epoxide
A cyclase converts the epoxide to lanosterol
20 more steps are required to form cholesterol
All at/in the endoplasmic reticulum membrane

Figure 24.35 This conversion requires FAD and NADPH as coenzymes and requires O 2 as well as a cytosolic protein called soluble protein activator.  A cyclase catalyzes the second reaction, which involves a succession of 1,2 shifts of hydride ions and methyl groups.
Figure 24.35. Twenty steps are required to convert lanosterol to cholesterol The enzymes ar all associated with the endoplasmic reticulum.

Statins Lower Serum Cholesterol Levels
HMG-CoA reductase is the rate-limiting step in cholesterol synthesis.  As such, it is a likely drug target. Mevinolin and similar drugs are cholesterol-lowering drugs that act as transition-state analogs, binding like transition states to the HMG-CoA reductase .

Statins Lower Serum Cholesterol Levels
The structure of mevinolinic acid compared with the structure of mevalonate and the tetrahedral intermediate in the mechanism of HMG-CoA reductase.  Mevinolin is converted to mevinolinic acid (a transition-state analog) in the body.

Statins Lower Serum Cholesterol Levels
For several years, Lipitor was the best-selling drug in the world, with  annual sales exceeding $10 billion.  The patent rights for Lipitor have now expired, and generic versions are available.
Statins Lower Serum Cholesterol Levels
Statins Lower Serum Cholesterol Levels
Cholesterol: precursor for steroids

Steroid Hormones
A hormone is a molecule that is synthesized in one part of an organism, which then elicits a response at a different site.
Two important classes of steroid hormones include sex hormones and adrenal cortical steroids .
The female sex hormones are estrogens and progestins .
The male sex hormones are called androgens .

The estrogens estradiol and estrone control development of secondary sex characteristics, regulate the menstrual cycle, and are made in the ovaries.
Steroid Hormones Female Sex Hormones: Estrogens

Steroid Hormones Female Sex Hormones: Progestin
The progestin progesterone is called the “ pregnancy hormone ” ; it is responsible for the  preparation of the uterus for implantation of a  fertilized egg.

Steroid Hormones Male Sex Hormones: Androgens
Testosterone and androsterone are androgens made in the testes.
They control the development of secondary sex characteristics in males .

Synthetic androgens
Synthetic androgen analogues, called anabolic steroids , promote muscle growth.
They have the same effect as testosterone , but are more stable, so they are not metabolized as quickly.
They have come to be used by athletes and body builders , but are not permitted in competitive  sports.
Prolonged use of anabolic steroids can cause physical and psychological problems .
Synthetic steroids

Steroid Hormones Adrenal Cortical Steroids
Three examples of adrenal cortical steroids are:
aldosterone
cortisone
cortisol

Steroid Hormones Adrenal Cortical Steroids
Aldosterone regulates blood pressure and volume by controlling the concentration of Na + and K + in body fluids.
Cortisone and cortisol serve as anti-inflammatory agents, which also regulate carbohydrate  metabolism.
Prednisone , a synthetic alternative, has similar anti-inflammatory properties.
